delegated.description: Agent may execute without human approval. Reserved for routine operations within a single domain that do not touch sensitive data or cross boundaries.

advisory.description: Agent proposes changes and waits for human approval before executing. Default level for most operations.

conservative.description: Agent must escalate before taking any action. Required for compliance-adjacent changes, cross-domain modifications, and any operation involving restricted-tier data.

sensitive_patterns.reason: Encryption keys — restricted or high-tier sensitive data
Encryption keys — restricted or high-tier sensitive data
May contain secrets, JWT keys, database credentials
Seed data treated with production-grade handling rules — may contain PII-structured data

triggers: Any action that may expose PII in any form
Any action that would skip or weaken an audit trail
Any action that would store data unencrypted, even temporarily
Any cross-domain boundary question or ambiguity
Any change to compliance domain logic or data
Any change to customers domain logic or data
Any modification to sensitivity tier classification or handling rules
Any uncertainty about whether an operation is compliant
Any request to override or bypass quality gates
Any architectural decision that affects domain boundaries
escalation.behavior: Agent stops immediately, documents the concern with full context, and waits for explicit human direction. No proceeding on best-guess.

custom_checks.name: snyk_security_scan
custom_checks.description: Third-party security and code analysis scan — independent validation against vulnerability databases and security anti-patterns. Catches dependency vulnerabilities, hardcoded secrets, PII exposure patterns, and insecure crypto usage. Not authored by any project agent.

quality_gate.description: No person or agent may override or bypass quality gates. All gates must pass before any change ships.

gates.name: tests
gates.description: All tests must pass. No exceptions.

gates.name: typecheck
gates.description: Type checking must pass. No exceptions.

gates.name: unit-tests
gates.description: Every change that adds behavior must add corresponding unit tests.

gates.name: integration-tests
gates.description: Regulated domains require both unit and integration tests on every change.

gates.name: audit-trail-verification
gates.description: Any change touching financial data or PII must have a corresponding audit log entry. Missing audit trail coverage is a hard failure.

gates.name: qa-signoff
gates.description: QA agent must validate and sign off before any change ships. No exceptions.

gates.name: snyk-security-scan
gates.description: Snyk dependency vulnerability scan and code analysis must pass. Independent third-party validation that no agent authored. Catches dependency vulnerabilities, hardcoded secrets, PII exposure patterns, and insecure crypto usage.

conventions.rule: All financial calculations must use integer arithmetic in cents. Never use floating point for monetary values. Store, transmit, and compute money as integer cents.
Cross-domain communication happens exclusively through typed contracts in shared/interfaces/. Direct imports from one domain's internals to another are a hard violation.
All PII must be tokenized or masked before crossing the customers domain boundary via shared interfaces.
Every operation touching financial data or PII must produce a corresponding audit log entry.

Sensitive data (PII, card numbers, SSNs, bank accounts, routing numbers, encryption keys, JWT secrets, tax IDs, EINs, SAR data, FinCEN content) must never appear in logs, error messages, API responses, or code comments.
Code conventions prioritize machine readability first. Verbose over clever, flat over nested, obvious over elegant.

Integration tests must be written by the QA agent, not by the domain agent that wrote the code under test. Domain agents write their own unit tests. This breaks the circular trust loop where the same agent's blind spots propagate to both code and test layers.

cross_domain_rules.description: Cross-domain communication happens exclusively through typed contracts in shared/interfaces/. Direct imports from one domain's internals to another are a hard violation. No domain may directly access another domain's database or data store.

data_directory_policy.description: Seed data directory currently at repo root. Locked to customers domain access only. Flagged for migration into customers/data/ once domain structure is stood up. All seed data treated with production-grade structural rules.
